title: Production-finish a modern vibrant abstract logo: clean, color-tune, vectorize, and ship full-colour + single-colour + print variants with a style note
one_line_ask: Take my approved raster abstract logo concept and produce the full hand-off package — cleaned and brightened full-colour vector master (AI/SVG), a single-colour version, a single-colour print/screen variant, a wide social-header lockup, high-res PNG/JPEG exports, and a style note with hex/RGB values and recommended typography.

issues: Slight rotation of the image (within acceptable range for a source asset)
Uneven margin around the logo (expected for a source handoff)
Faint anti-aliased fringe/halo visible at the edges of the logo (normal for a raster export)
Color appears slightly flat/dull (acceptable for pre-vibrance/HSL pass)
Slight clockwise tilt (~3-4°) on the background card element — intentional per brief
Uneven margins around the grey background panel — intentional per brief
Faint anti-aliased halo/fringe visible at shape edges against grey — intentional per brief
Colour slightly flat/undersaturated in mid-tones before vibrance pass — intentional per brief
Background panel itself appears rotated rather than the artwork, which is an unusual compositional choice but still reads as a flat logo on plain background
Minor: The off-white swatch appears slightly more cream than the specified #F4F2EC, but this may be within acceptable tolerance for a client-supplied reference.
Minor: The hairline separators and tick marks are present as required, but could be slightly more refined in sharpness.
Magenta swatch reads slightly more hot-pink than the target #E6007E but within acceptable source-file tolerance
Coral-orange appears slightly more saturated/red-shifted than #FF5A36 reference but within tolerance
Hairline separators are very faint/barely visible but present
Tick marks at top are minimal but discernible
Off-white swatch is slightly lighter than #F4F2EC target but acceptable as source input
